title: Notes on Persian rose poems
notes: The gol is never just a flower. In Rumi it is the wound that doesn't stop opening. In Hafez it is the cup that doesn't stop being lifted. The English translations almost always pick one and lose the other. The work is to keep both at once.
next_action: Choose three pairs (Rumi/Hafez) and write side-by-side, with the original above. Aim for 800 words total.
